title: SAF Drone Strikes Kill 24+ Civilians in Um Dukhun Market (Central Darfur) and North Kordofan in Single Day
detail: Sudanese Armed Forces drone strikes killed at least 24 people and injured approximately 20 more in Um Dukhun town, Central Darfur, on April 17, 2026. The strikes hit a popular market, a fuel market, and residential neighborhoods in the Buram locality near the Chad border — an area under RSF control. Medical sources in the area reported an entire family of six was killed and that more than 10 additional people had severely burned bodies. Parallel strikes on markets in North Kordofan on the same day brought the combined one-day death toll to at least 44 civilians across both regions, according to Radio Dabanga. The strikes continued the pattern of SAF long-range drone operations against RSF-held civilian areas documented throughout early 2026; since January, UN-documented drone strikes in the Kordofan and Darfur regions have killed approximately 700 civilians including 245 children in Q1 2026 alone (per Amnesty International). Um Dukhun is located near major humanitarian access routes to Central African Republic and Chad, and its market served as a critical supply point for communities throughout Buram locality. The attacks drew renewed condemnation from UN human rights bodies as evidence of systematic targeting of civilian economic infrastructure.

media.caption: Drone strikes kill 24+ civilians in Um Dukhun market area in Central Darfur on April 17, 2026

title: Nuba Mountains: A Fragile Refuge on Sudan's Front Line
detail: A DW field report from the Nuba Mountains in South Kordofan highlights the region as a precarious refuge for civilians caught between SAF and RSF front lines. The mountainous terrain has historically sheltered displaced populations and resistance fighters, but worsening fighting has eroded that protection. Humanitarian access remains severely constrained, and communities face acute food insecurity and lack of medical care as the conflict grinds into its fourth year.

media.caption: Nuba Mountains — a fragile refuge on Sudan's front line
